description: A 'Worst of the Week' rogues-gallery infographic on a black textured background. Twelve real booking/mugshot-style photographs of men are arranged in a grid (four across the top, single columns down the left and right of a central title, and four across the bottom). Each headshot has a gray caption bar beneath it naming an alleged crime: 'Sexual Assault,' 'Aggravated Assault,' 'Child Abuse,' 'Child Sexual Assault,' 'Child Rape,' 'Fentanyl Possession,' 'Cocaine Possession,' 'Rape,' 'Indecency w/ Child,' 'Aggravated Assault,' 'Firearm Possession,' 'Child Sexual Exploitation.' Centered is a bold white serif/sans logo lockup reading 'Worst' with a red parallelogram banner 'of the' and 'Week.' Bottom left is a red bar with white text 'ice.gov/WoW'; bottom right shows the circular DHS seal and the 'ICE' wordmark. The men appear to be of varied ethnicities. Propaganda technique: aggregates mugshots and severe charge labels (heavy emphasis on child sex offenses and drugs) into a recurring branded series that frames immigrants as predators, encouraging followers to 'share' and visit the agency's web page. Target audience: the general public, to build fear-based support for mass enforcement.
notable_text: Worst of the Week; ice.gov/WoW; Sexual Assault; Child Abuse; Child Rape; Fentanyl Possession; Rape; Child Sexual Exploitation; ICE
